dailyJudgementSnippet: OpenAI edged out the field by attracting only one clearly relevant complaint-heavy discussion, centered on GPT-5/o3 agent economics, while Anthropic took broader criticism across Opus 4.7 and Claude Design and Microsoft Copilot picked up a pricing-related complaint [E4][E2][E3][E5].
winnerExplanation: OpenAI won because its direct HN signal was narrower than the others: commenters were negative about GPT-5 and o3 costs, but that was a single concentrated story [E4]. Anthropic had more total visibility, yet the extra attention came with heavier downside: Opus 4.7 was criticized for burning limits faster without obvious gains, and Claude Design drew complaints about exhausting weekly usage too quickly [E2][E3]. Microsoft Copilot's only meaningful signal was also negative, tied to a perceived price/performance downgrade inside VSCode Copilot [E5].

openai.judgementSnippet: Discussion centered on agent economics: commenters highlighted GPT-5 and o3 as expensive per productive hour, with some arguing humans are already cheaper for many tasks [E4].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude Code got real credit for handling a messy server migration, but that was outweighed by complaints that Opus 4.7 consumes limits faster without obvious gains and that Claude Design burns through weekly usage too quickly to feel practical [E1][E2][E3].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: The main Copilot signal was a complaint that VSCode Copilot will swap in Opus 4.7 with a 7.5x modifier, which read to users as a price hike for slower, more overthinking behavior [E5].

evidence.summary: In comments on the cloud-migration story, one user said Claude Code migrated two messy servers with obsolete libraries and rewrote missing pieces, making a complex move much easier.
Comments on the Opus 4.6/4.7 comparison said 4.7's capability bump was hard to notice, usage limits were consumed much faster, and Anthropic seemed too focused on token spending.
Comments on Claude Design questioned the product's usefulness, with one user using 95% of weekly design allowance after iterative tweaking and calling it more toy than tool.
Comments on AI-agent costs repeatedly singled out GPT-5 and o3 as very expensive per productive hour, including claims that humans are already cheaper on many tasks.
A commenter said VSCode Copilot will replace Opus 4.5/4.6 with 4.7 at a 7.5x modifier, making Copilot feel like a price increase for slower, more overthinking behavior.
